result: If I could change the world in a single way, I would make empathy and compassion the default lens through which people see one another. I think that if everyone routinely tried to understand each other's perspectives and acted out of genuine concern for each other's well‑being, most of the other problems we face—conflict, inequality, environmental destruction, political polarization—would become far easier to solve. A world guided by deep empathy would naturally prioritize collaboration over competition, sustainability over exploitation, and justice over indifference. It’s a simple shift in mindset, but its ripple effects could transform everything from personal relationships to global policies.
